title: Green Discharge During Dog Pregnancy: When to Seek Emergency Care
meta_desc: Seeing green discharge from your pregnant dog? This is a high-priority emergency sign. Learn why immediate veterinary care is vital for her safety.
content_html:
What is Green Discharge During Pregnancy?

Green discharge in a pregnant dog is usually a pigment called uteroverdin. This pigment is released when the placenta separates from the uterine wall. While it is a normal part of the birthing process once labor has officially begun, seeing it at the wrong time or without the arrival of a puppy is a major cause for concern.

Is It an Emergency?

Yes, this is considered a high-urgency medical emergency. If you see green discharge and no puppy is born within 15 to 30 minutes, it indicates that the puppies' oxygen supply is being compromised because the placenta has detached. This situation often points to fetal distress, a stalled labor, or a potential infection, and requires immediate veterinary intervention to save the lives of the mother and the pups.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

When you call your veterinarian or an emergency clinic, having a clear photo of the discharge can be extremely beneficial. A photo allows the medical team to visually confirm the color, viscosity, and amount of the discharge. This helps the triage nurse prioritize your case and prepare the necessary diagnostic tools or surgical equipment before you even walk through the door.

faqs.question: Is green discharge ever considered normal?
faqs.answer: It is only normal if a puppy is delivered almost immediately (within 30 minutes) after the discharge appears. If no puppy arrives, it is a sign of placental separation and a veterinary emergency.

faqs.question: What if my dog is acting fine but has green discharge?
faqs.answer: Even if your dog is acting normally, green discharge is a physiological sign that the puppies are losing their life-support system. Do not wait for her to show signs of pain or illness.

faqs.question: Should I wait until morning to call the vet?
faqs.answer: No. If you see green discharge, you should contact an emergency 24-hour veterinary facility immediately, as fetal survival depends on rapid intervention.
